pattern: A chessboard square's color is exactly the parity of its column-plus-row index sum, so two squares match colors precisely when those two parities agree.
hints: Convert the column letter to a zero-based index and the row character to its digit value.
A square is one color when the sum of its column index and row value is even, and the other color when that sum is odd.
Compare the parity of that sum for both coordinates directly, with no need to track which color is which.

edge_inputs.description: two squares of the same color
adjacent columns on the same row always differ in color
same column, adjacent rows always differ in color

stress.description: random pairs of valid chessboard coordinates
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    c1 = chr(ord('a') + rng.randint(0, 7)) + str(rng.randint(1, 8))
    c2 = chr(ord('a') + rng.randint(0, 7)) + str(rng.randint(1, 8))
    return [c1, c2]
stress.note: covers every one of the 64 squares uniformly over many trials
